project.title: Medevac Ambulancias
project.one_liner: Legacy PHP web application for Medevac Ambulancias, combining a public services website with an administrative sales, CRM, and inventory panel.
project.description: The repository contains a public-facing multi-page website for ambulance and medical support services plus a password-protected admin panel for catalog management, customer records, carts, point-of-sale operations, scheduling, permissions, and live chat handling.

prerequisites: MySQL database with the expected application tables
setup_steps: This repository does not include a one-command local setup.
Serve the project from a PHP-capable web root so `index.html`, `php/`, `correo/`, `achat/`, and `panel/` are reachable.
Configure the database connection used by `panel/admin/classes/permisos.php` before using the admin panel.
Open `index.html` for the public site or `panel/index.html` for the admin login once backend dependencies are available.

cv_bullets.bullet: Built a multi-page public website for an ambulance-services business, covering emergency care, transfers, neonatal therapy, event coverage, medical consultation, training, outsourcing, gallery, customer feedback, and invoice requests.
cv_bullets.notes: The public navigation and page set explicitly list these service areas and customer-facing flows.

cv_bullets.bullet: Implemented an embedded website chat that can open live conversations, poll for new messages, and fall back to deferred inquiry capture when operators are unavailable.
cv_bullets.notes: Availability checks, chat creation, message polling, and admin-side retrieval are all present in code.

cv_bullets.bullet: Built AJAX-backed invoice-request and feedback flows that submit structured form data to PHP mail handlers.
cv_bullets.notes: Both public forms post to the same mail endpoint, which changes subject/recipient behavior based on the payload.

cv_bullets.bullet: Delivered a role-aware admin panel with login, session tracking, and permission-gated tabs and actions.
cv_bullets.notes: The admin area includes credential-based login, session persistence, and a jsTree-driven permission model.

cv_bullets.bullet: Created catalog and product-management workflows spanning categories, nested subcategories, discounts, suppliers, product metadata, attachments, images, and inventory thresholds.
cv_bullets.notes: The UI and backend classes explicitly support these entities and related upload/linking flows.

cv_bullets.bullet: Implemented cart and point-of-sale flows with barcode entry, mixed payment methods, invoice flags, saved/open carts, and sales reporting.
cv_bullets.notes: The panel exposes dedicated POS, cart, and account-balance flows, and backend code validates inventory before finalizing sales.

cv_bullets.bullet: Built CRM-oriented customer workflows covering profile capture, multiple phone numbers, billing and shipping data, follow-up calls, and Excel-based imports.
cv_bullets.notes: Customer forms, call logging, and spreadsheet ingestion are explicitly implemented.

cv_bullets.bullet: Added agenda and calendar features for customer-related tasks, including pending/completed states and in-app reminders.
cv_bullets.notes: The panel loads a FullCalendar view and uses agenda endpoints plus reminder notifications for scheduled activity.

cv_bullets.bullet: Localized the admin experience for Spanish-speaking operators, including Spanish language packs and Spanish-first UI copy across dashboards and forms.
cv_bullets.notes: The admin shell loads Spanish locale files and the application text is written in Spanish.

cv_bullets.bullet: Implemented supplier onboarding with tokenized access links and category-assignment workflows inside the admin panel.
cv_bullets.notes: Supplier records can be created, linked to categories, and emailed an embedded-access token.

red_flags.issue: The repository root in the provided environment context did not match the actual git checkout location.
red_flags.notes: The git repository was found at D:/desarrollo-respaldo/AppServ/www/portfolio-ambulancias, not at the initial D:/desarrollo-respaldo/AppServ/www/ambulancias path.

red_flags.issue: Database and SMTP configuration are hardcoded in source files, which is risky for public repositories and deployment hygiene.
red_flags.notes: Secret values were intentionally not copied into the README or this JSON output.

red_flags.issue: The codebase relies on deprecated PHP APIs such as `mysql_*`, `ereg/eregi`, and `get_magic_quotes_gpc`, which will require modernization on current PHP versions.
red_flags.notes: These APIs were removed from modern PHP releases.

red_flags.issue: One admin tab references a missing class file in this workspace.
red_flags.notes: `panel/tabs/clientes.php` includes `panel/admin/classes/usuarios.class.php`, but that file is not present in this tree.
